files.complete_text:
Prayer

By Carrie Tanner

A prayer is soul-sent wish, e'en whispered still,
By mystic power borne through vaulted blue
To that bright realm where only prayers most true
E'er reach the Majesty of sacred will.
He grants—or stays with purpose kind, until
In justice great, or mercy sweet, He heeds
Our humble wish and heavy mortal needs
With gracious love, and earthly measures fill
To overflow. How meager the estate
Of those who ne'er this sweetest joy doth know,
Whose hearts clutch fast grim avarice and hate,
Disdaining Him from whom all blessings flow.
O prayer that gives the soul a peace divine!
O prayer, dear privilege great, the gift is Thine!
files.title: Prayer
files.author: Carrie Tanner
files.notes: Found complete poem on page 3 of the PDF. The file only contained the last two lines; this is a complete 14-line sonnet.

files.complete_text:
Portrait of Grace Ingles Frost

Grace Ingles Frost of Provo, Utah, for a poem entitled "Compensation," which appears on the following page, was awarded first prize in the Eliza R. Snow Poetry Contest, conducted by the Relief Society Magazine.
files.title: Portrait of Grace Ingles Frost
files.notes: Found complete text on page 6 of PDF. This is a brief biographical note about Grace Ingles Frost winning the poetry contest, with her poem 'Compensation' appearing on the next page.
